nonNegotiables: Reuse the existing IssueSummaryDto response contract unchanged for PATCH responses.
Accept only title, description, addLabels, and removeLabels as mutable request fields.
Treat description empty string as an intentional clear and description null as no change.
Reject requests with no effective update field and reject overlapping addLabels/removeLabels values.
Use shared application.yml-backed title, description, and labels-per-field limits for both create and update.
Keep the local API as PATCH /api/issues/{issueId} while using GitLab PUT /projects/:id/issues/:issue_iid downstream.
Use GitLab add_labels and remove_labels rather than full labels replacement for additive label behavior.

scope.purpose: Add PATCH issue updates for title, description, and label deltas while aligning create validation with shared configurable limits and preserving the existing issue summary response contract.
inScope: Expose PATCH /api/issues/{issueId} in the existing issues capability and return HTTP 200 with the existing IssueSummaryDto response shape.
Add one REST-boundary validator in the existing orchestration/issues/rest package to own create and update payload validation backed by shared issues API properties.
Extend app.issues-api configuration with nested validation limits for title min length, title max length, description max length, and max labels per request field.
Introduce provider-agnostic update orchestration input and a new IssuesPort update method.
Map update requests to the official GitLab project issue update endpoint using title, description, add_labels, and remove_labels.
Update flow-orchestrator/http/issues.http, documentation/capabilities/issues.md, unit tests, integration tests, component tests, and the existing issues-update Karate smoke feature.
outOfScope: Updating issue fields other than title, description, addLabels, and removeLabels.
Changing issue search, delete, get-single, or label-event behavior beyond the shared create/update validation alignment.
Introducing a new issue response DTO or changing IssueSummaryDto field names, wire values, or null-inclusion behavior.
Adding project selection per request, batch updates, or any read-before-write reconciliation flow.
Introducing a new top-level package or a new issues subpackage boundary just for validation.
constraints: Source of truth remains artifacts/user-prompts/update-issue-api.md and artifacts/user-stories/update-issue-api.story.md approved in flow-log summary on 2026-04-19.
Official GitLab contract is PUT /projects/:id/issues/:issue_iid with at least one mutable parameter and supported add_labels/remove_labels fields per https://docs.gitlab.com/api/issues/.
The update success response must keep using the current IssueSummary -> IssueSummaryDto mapping path with assignee, milestone, parent, and nullable changeSets preserved exactly as today.
IssueSummaryDto.changeSets remains nullable and omitted from JSON when null via existing @JsonInclude(NON_NULL); update responses do not introduce a different omission rule.
Shared infrastructure reuse is mandatory: keep using GitLabUriFactory, GitLabExceptionMapper, IssuesResponseMapper, and the existing issues capability package layout.
Because the validator remains in the existing orchestration/issues/rest package, the plan does not introduce a new package relationship and therefore does not require a new ArchUnit rule.
Create validation alignment is part of the same delivery and must use the same configured limits consumed by PATCH validation.

validationBoundaryDecision.decision: Own create and update request validation in a dedicated IssuesRequestValidator class that lives directly in orchestration/issues/rest, while keeping search field validation and path-variable annotations unchanged.
details: CreateIssueRequest and UpdateIssueRequest stay transport-only and allow deserialization to preserve null values; the validator decides whether those values are acceptable.
IssuesController calls IssuesRequestValidator before request-to-input mapping for create and patch flows.
IssuesRequestValidator throws ValidationException with aggregated details so GlobalExceptionHandler continues shaping the outbound 400 response.
Title and description length rules use IssuesApiProperties.validation values instead of hard-coded Bean Validation annotations.
The validator treats title as effective only when non-null, description as effective only when non-null, and add/remove label lists as effective only when non-null and non-empty.
Search endpoint validation and positive issueId path validation continue using the current mechanisms because those rules are already declarative and unchanged by this feature.

compositionStrategy.sequentialDependency: The update flow has no independent downstream calls to parallelize. Validation happens first, then one orchestration delegation leads to one GitLab PUT call.
compositionStrategy.failureMode: Any GitLab HTTP or transport failure is translated by the existing GitLabExceptionMapper path for the update issue resource and returned through the existing GlobalExceptionHandler contract.

reused: config/IssuesApiProperties as the existing app.issues-api root configuration object
integration/gitlab/GitLabExceptionMapper for sanitized GitLab error translation

assumptions: GitLab project issue update responses contain the same issue summary fields already mapped by GitLabIssueResponse, so update can reuse the existing GitLabIssueResponse -> IssueSummary mapper path.
Issue identifiers used by the local API remain GitLab issue IIDs, so the downstream path segment is the same project-scoped identifier already used by delete and get-single flows.

The existing issues-update Karate feature file in src/test/karate/resources/issues should be modified to match the final implementation rather than replaced with a new runner or a second update feature.

implementationFlow.responsibility: Extend app.issues-api with nested immutable validation limits and startup validation while preserving the fixed 20/40 pagination contract.
implementationFlow.constitutionReason: Principle 5 requires centralized validated configuration in the config layer.
outputs: IssuesApiProperties exposes a non-null validation component.
Shared title, description, and labels-per-field limits are available to both create and update flows.

implementationFlow.responsibility: Move create request validation from hard-coded transport annotations to IssuesRequestValidator and keep create defensive-copy behavior intact.
implementationFlow.constitutionReason: Principles 4, 8, and 12 require boundary validation before business logic and explicit ownership of transport rules.
outputs: Create requests use the shared configured limits.
Create validation errors still surface through ValidationException and the existing error response shape.

implementationFlow.responsibility: Add UpdateIssueRequest, PATCH /api/issues/{issueId}, and request-to-input mapping while keeping the controller thin and reusing the existing IssueSummaryDto mapper.
implementationFlow.constitutionReason: Principles 1 and 8 require the controller to validate, delegate, map, and return without provider-specific behavior.
outputs: PATCH /api/issues/{issueId} exists with request-body and path validation.
UpdateIssueRequest semantics preserve null-versus-empty behavior required by the locked contract.

implementationFlow.responsibility: Add focused boundary tests for update validation, mapping, and HTTP response behavior, including overlap rejection and empty-description semantics.
implementationFlow.constitutionReason: Principle 10 requires changed validation and mapping behavior to be covered at the smallest effective levels.
outputs: Focused unit and WebMvc coverage for PATCH request behavior.
Evidence that overlap validation short-circuits before downstream delegation.

implementationFlow.responsibility: Add UpdateIssueInput and IssuesPort.updateIssue, then delegate update work through IssuesService with structured logging and no read-before-write composition.
implementationFlow.constitutionReason: Principles 2 and 13 require orchestration to define the provider-agnostic contract and keep provider mechanics out of the REST layer.
outputs: IssuesPort exposes a single provider-agnostic update capability.

implementationFlow.responsibility: Map UpdateIssueInput to a GitLab PUT request that serializes add_labels and remove_labels as comma-separated strings, preserves description='', and reuses the existing GitLabIssueResponse mapper output.
implementationFlow.constitutionReason: Principles 2 and 8 require one external call per adapter method and explicit mapping at the integration boundary.
outputs: The update response maps back to IssueSummary without a new provider response DTO.

implementationFlow.responsibility: Update issues.http and documentation/capabilities/issues.md so the local PATCH contract and shared validation configuration stay synchronized with implementation.
implementationFlow.constitutionReason: Principle 11 requires implementation and integration documentation to change in the same delivery.
outputs: HTTP examples show PATCH success and validation failures.
Issues capability docs list PATCH and the shared validation contract.

implementationFlow.responsibility: Extend the existing update smoke feature and component test support so PATCH behavior is verified against a running application and WireMock-backed GitLab responses.
implementationFlow.constitutionReason: Code-guidance final verification requires API-facing changes to have smoke coverage, and test-instructions require component coverage for changed real downstream HTTP flow.
outputs: IssuesApiComponentTest covers PATCH success and downstream failure behavior.
issues-update.feature covers a valid PATCH flow and at least one invalid PATCH request.

contractExamples.label: Create request using shared validation-backed fields
PATCH request with title, description, and label delta
PATCH request that intentionally clears description
GitLab not-found error response mapped through existing error contract
Validation error when addLabels and removeLabels overlap

details: addLabels and removeLabels must not contain the same value: bug

validationRules.rule: Create title must be non-null, non-blank, and its length must be between issuesApiProperties.validation.titleMinLength and issuesApiProperties.validation.titleMaxLength inclusive.
validationRules.reason: The locked request explicitly aligns create title validation with the shared configurable limits.

validationRules.rule: Create description may be null but, when present, must not exceed issuesApiProperties.validation.descriptionMaxLength.
validationRules.reason: Create and update must share one description max-length source.

validationRules.rule: Create labels, when present, must contain at most issuesApiProperties.validation.maxLabelsPerRequestField items and every item must be non-null and non-blank.
validationRules.reason: The locked request requires labels-per-field alignment across create and update.

validationRules.rule: Update requests must contain at least one effective change: title non-null, description non-null, addLabels non-empty, or removeLabels non-empty.
validationRules.reason: Both the local PATCH contract and the GitLab update endpoint require at least one mutable field.

validationRules.rule: Update title, when provided, must be non-blank and its length must be between issuesApiProperties.validation.titleMinLength and issuesApiProperties.validation.titleMaxLength inclusive.
validationRules.reason: PATCH title rules are explicitly locked and must reuse the shared configured limits.

validationRules.rule: Update description, when provided, may be the empty string but must not exceed issuesApiProperties.validation.descriptionMaxLength; null means no change and must not be sent as an effective update field.
validationRules.reason: The request explicitly distinguishes empty string from null for PATCH description semantics.

validationRules.rule: addLabels, when provided, must contain at most issuesApiProperties.validation.maxLabelsPerRequestField items and each item must be non-null and non-blank.
validationRules.reason: The request explicitly caps additive labels and requires meaningful non-blank values.

validationRules.rule: removeLabels, when provided, must contain at most issuesApiProperties.validation.maxLabelsPerRequestField items and each item must be non-null and non-blank.
validationRules.reason: The request explicitly caps removal labels and requires meaningful non-blank values.

validationRules.rule: Update requests must reject exact-string overlap between addLabels and removeLabels and report the conflicting value list in the validation details.
validationRules.reason: The user explicitly requires rejection when the same label appears in both operations.

designDecisions.title: Validation Boundary Decision
designDecisions.decision: Create a dedicated IssuesRequestValidator class in com.gitlabflow.floworchestrator.orchestration.issues.rest and call it from IssuesController for create and patch requests before request-to-input mapping.
designDecisions.rationale: Config-backed lengths, at-least-one-field validation, and cross-list overlap rules are not cleanly expressible with the current field-level annotations. Keeping the validator in the existing rest package keeps the controller thin without introducing a new package boundary that would need separate architectural enforcement.
alternatives: Keep create annotations and add manual checks only for PATCH: rejected because rule ownership would stay split and create would not truly share the configurable limits.
Add a new orchestration/issues/rest/validation package plus an ArchUnit rule: rejected because one validator class does not justify a new package boundary in this capability.

designDecisions.title: Shared validation configuration
designDecisions.decision: Add IssuesApiValidationProperties as a nested component of the existing IssuesApiProperties record under app.issues-api.validation while preserving default-page-size and max-page-size property names.
designDecisions.rationale: The issues capability already owns app.issues-api. Nesting the validation settings there keeps all issue API defaults together and satisfies the requirement for shared configuration-backed create/update limits.
alternatives: Hard-code the new limits in request DTO annotations: rejected because the request requires application.yml configurability.
Create a second top-level issues validation prefix: rejected because it would fragment one capability's configuration across two roots.

designDecisions.title: Update orchestration contract
designDecisions.decision: Introduce UpdateIssueInput as the only new orchestration input model and add IssueSummary updateIssue(UpdateIssueInput input) to IssuesPort and IssuesService, while reusing existing IssueSummary and IssueSummaryDto unchanged for success output.
designDecisions.rationale: PATCH needs a provider-agnostic write contract carrying issueId plus optional fields. Reusing IssueSummary and IssueSummaryDto preserves the locked response contract and avoids operation-specific response variants.
alternatives: Pass UpdateIssueRequest directly into integration: rejected because it leaks REST transport concerns past the boundary.
Create a new update response DTO: rejected because the request explicitly forbids it.

designDecisions.title: GitLab update mapping
designDecisions.decision: Add GitLabUpdateIssueRequest with wire fields title, description, add_labels, and remove_labels. GitLabIssuesMapper serializes add/remove label lists as comma-separated strings, omits null or empty add/remove values, and preserves description='' so GitLab clears the description when requested.
designDecisions.rationale: GitLab's official update issue API is PUT with add_labels/remove_labels. Using those provider fields preserves additive label behavior and avoids a second read-before-write call.
alternatives: Use the labels replacement field: rejected because it would replace the full label set instead of applying deltas.
Fetch the issue first and compute a replacement labels string: rejected because it adds latency and violates the one-call-per-adapter-method pattern for this flow.

designDecisions.title: Composition Strategy
designDecisions.decision: Keep the update flow strictly sequential and single-call: REST validation, request mapping, service delegation, one GitLab PUT call, then response mapping.
designDecisions.rationale: There are no independent downstream calls to parallelize. GitLab already returns the updated issue body, so read-after-write composition adds no business value.
alternatives: Pre-fetch the issue to verify existence: rejected because GitLab already returns 404 on update and the extra call adds latency.
Update then re-read the issue: rejected because the update response already contains the reusable summary fields needed for IssueSummaryDto.

designDecisions.title: Shared infrastructure and architecture-test impact
designDecisions.decision: Reuse the existing IssuesResponseMapper, GitLabUriFactory, GitLabExceptionMapper, IssuesKarateTest runner, and issues capability documentation artifact. Do not add a new ArchUnit rule because the revised plan keeps validation in the existing orchestration/issues/rest package and does not introduce a new layer interaction or package boundary.
designDecisions.rationale: This feature extends an existing capability and reuses existing boundaries. The prior ArchUnit concern came from a proposed new rest.validation package; that package is no longer part of the plan, so no new structural rule is needed.
alternatives: Introduce a new validation subpackage and add an ArchUnit rule for it: rejected because the package split adds ceremony without architectural value for a single class.
Extract executeGitLabOperation to shared integration infrastructure now: rejected because architecture guidance says to extract it when a second adapter needs the pattern.

models.purpose: Central immutable configuration for issues capability pagination and shared create/update validation limits.
models.justification: Belongs in config because Constitution Principle 5 requires centralized validated configuration objects in the config layer.
models.placementJustification: Belongs in config because Constitution Principle 5 requires centralized validated configuration objects in the config layer.

fields.name: defaultPageSize
fields.notes: Existing fixed contract value remains 20.

fields.name: maxPageSize
fields.notes: Existing fixed contract value remains 40.

fields.name: validation
fields.notes: New nested configuration object bound from app.issues-api.validation.* and required to be non-null.

models.purpose: Holds shared create/update payload limits for title, description, and labels-per-field.
models.justification: This is immutable startup configuration, so it belongs in config rather than orchestration or REST packages.
models.placementJustification: This is immutable startup configuration, so it belongs in config rather than orchestration or REST packages.

fields.name: titleMinLength
fields.notes: Default application.yml value: 3.

fields.name: titleMaxLength
fields.notes: Default application.yml value: 255 and must be greater than or equal to titleMinLength.

fields.name: descriptionMaxLength
fields.notes: Default application.yml value: 1000000.

fields.name: maxLabelsPerRequestField
fields.notes: Default application.yml value: 10 and applies separately to labels, addLabels, and removeLabels.

models.purpose: REST request DTO for POST /api/issues after moving create payload validation into IssuesRequestValidator.
models.justification: This remains an HTTP transport contract and therefore belongs in orchestration/issues/rest/dto.
models.placementJustification: This remains an HTTP transport contract and therefore belongs in orchestration/issues/rest/dto.

fields.name: title
fields.notes: Jackson may supply null when the field is absent; IssuesRequestValidator rejects null and blank values.

fields.name: description
fields.notes: Null means omitted; empty string remains allowed.

fields.name: labels
fields.notes: Compact constructor preserves null and defensively copies provided non-null elements without changing list order.

models.purpose: REST request DTO for PATCH /api/issues/{issueId} carrying optional update fields and label delta lists.
models.justification: This is an HTTP boundary payload and belongs in orchestration/issues/rest/dto to keep transport concerns out of orchestration and integration models.
models.placementJustification: This is an HTTP boundary payload and belongs in orchestration/issues/rest/dto to keep transport concerns out of orchestration and integration models.

fields.name: title
fields.notes: Null means no title change; blank is rejected by IssuesRequestValidator.

fields.name: description
fields.notes: Null means no change; empty string means explicit clear.

fields.name: addLabels
fields.notes: Compact constructor defensively copies provided values and preserves null elements so validator logic can reject them explicitly.

fields.name: removeLabels
fields.notes: Compact constructor defensively copies provided values and preserves null elements so validator logic can reject them explicitly.

models.purpose: Provider-agnostic orchestration input for create operations.
models.justification: Already correctly placed in orchestration/issues/model as a capability-local input contract.
models.placementJustification: Already correctly placed in orchestration/issues/model as a capability-local input contract.

fields.name: labels
fields.notes: Normalized to immutable empty list when absent.

models.purpose: Provider-agnostic orchestration input for issue update operations.
models.justification: This is business-intent input for the issues capability, so it belongs in orchestration/issues/model rather than in REST or integration.
models.placementJustification: This is business-intent input for the issues capability, so it belongs in orchestration/issues/model rather than in REST or integration.

fields.name: issueId
fields.notes: Project-scoped issue iid used by existing issues API paths.

fields.name: title
fields.notes: Null means no title change.

fields.name: description
fields.notes: Null means no change; empty string is preserved for downstream clear semantics.

fields.name: addLabels
fields.notes: Null request lists are normalized to immutable empty lists after validation decides whether they are effective.

fields.name: removeLabels
fields.notes: Null request lists are normalized to immutable empty lists after validation decides whether they are effective.

models.purpose: Provider-agnostic orchestration port for issue operations.
models.justification: Ports must live in orchestration per Constitution Principle 2 and existing issues capability structure.
models.placementJustification: Ports must live in orchestration per Constitution Principle 2 and existing issues capability structure.

models.purpose: Entity-shaped orchestration output reused for search, create, and update operations.
models.justification: Already correctly placed as the issues capability output model and reused unchanged by the update feature.
models.placementJustification: Already correctly placed as the issues capability output model and reused unchanged by the update feature.

fields.name: labels
fields.notes: Compact constructor normalizes null to immutable empty list.

fields.name: assignee
fields.notes: Single display username derived from assignees or deprecated assignee field.

fields.name: milestone
fields.notes: Milestone title when GitLab returns a milestone.

fields.name: parent
fields.notes: Epic id when GitLab returns epic information.

fields.name: changeSets
fields.notes: Null means no audit enrichment was applied; compact constructor defensively copies when present. Create and update flows keep this field null.

models.purpose: Shared REST response DTO reused unchanged by search, create, and patch update responses.
models.justification: The response contract remains a REST boundary type in orchestration/issues/rest/dto; the request explicitly requires reuse instead of a new DTO.
models.placementJustification: The response contract remains a REST boundary type in orchestration/issues/rest/dto; the request explicitly requires reuse instead of a new DTO.

fields.name: labels
fields.notes: Compact constructor normalizes null to immutable empty list.

fields.name: changeSets
fields.notes: Annotated with @JsonInclude(JsonInclude.Include.NON_NULL); when null it is omitted from JSON, which is the unchanged behavior required for create and update responses.

models.purpose: GitLab provider request DTO for the issue update endpoint.
models.justification: Provider-specific wire fields belong in integration/gitlab/issues/dto per Constitution Principle 2 and code-guidance package rules.
models.placementJustification: Provider-specific wire fields belong in integration/gitlab/issues/dto per Constitution Principle 2 and code-guidance package rules.

fields.name: title
fields.notes: Serialized as the wire field title when provided.

fields.name: description
fields.notes: Serialized as the wire field description; empty string must be preserved unchanged.

fields.name: addLabels
fields.notes: Annotated with @JsonProperty("add_labels"); null when no additive labels are provided, otherwise comma-separated label names.

fields.name: removeLabels
fields.notes: Annotated with @JsonProperty("remove_labels"); null when no removal labels are provided, otherwise comma-separated label names.

models.purpose: GitLab response DTO already used for issue list/create responses and reusable for the update response body.
models.justification: The provider response shape remains inside integration/gitlab/issues/dto and is reused unchanged by the adapter mapper.
models.placementJustification: The provider response shape remains inside integration/gitlab/issues/dto and is reused unchanged by the adapter mapper.

slices.title: Shared Config And Create Validation
slices.goal: Introduce the shared validation properties and align create-request validation with those limits without adding any new package boundary.
implementationTasks: Add IssuesApiValidationProperties and extend IssuesApiProperties with a required validation component plus startup guards for positive values and titleMaxLength >= titleMinLength.
Add default application.yml values under app.issues-api.validation.title-min-length, title-max-length, description-max-length, and max-labels-per-request-field.

Create IssuesRequestValidator in orchestration/issues/rest with validateCreateRequest(CreateIssueRequest request) and validateUpdateRequest(UpdateIssueRequest request) methods that collect all rule violations into ValidationException details.
Remove the create payload field annotations superseded by IssuesRequestValidator while preserving CreateIssueRequest list-copy behavior.
Update IssuesController create flow to call IssuesRequestValidator before mapping and extend tests to prove the create endpoint now uses shared configured limits.
doneWhen: Create requests reject null or blank title, out-of-range title length, too-long description, too many labels, and blank label values using IssuesApiProperties.validation values.
IssuesApiProperties fails startup for invalid nested validation configuration while keeping the existing 20/40 page-size contract.
CreateIssueRequest continues defensive-copy behavior and no longer owns business validation annotations for the shared rules.
At least one edge-case test proves a title boundary or labels-per-field boundary using the new shared configuration.

warn: None; validation warning logging remains centralized in GlobalExceptionHandler.

slices.title: PATCH Request Boundary
slices.goal: Add the PATCH transport contract, controller entry point, boundary validation, and request mapping for issue updates in a small independently verifiable increment.
implementationTasks: Add UpdateIssueRequest with nullable title, description, addLabels, and removeLabels fields plus defensive-copy behavior that preserves null elements for validator visibility.

Add IssuesRequestMapper.toUpdateIssueInput(long issueId, UpdateIssueRequest request) that preserves description='' and normalizes null add/remove lists to immutable empty lists in the orchestration input.

Extend IssuesController with @PatchMapping("/{issueId}") using @Positive path validation, IssuesRequestValidator.validateUpdateRequest, IssuesRequestMapper.toUpdateIssueInput, service delegation, and ResponseEntity.ok(IssueSummaryDto).
Extend IssuesRequestValidatorTest with update-specific unit coverage for empty payload, blank title, out-of-range lengths, over-limit labels, blank label values, and overlapping add/remove labels.
Extend IssuesControllerIT with PATCH success binding, PATCH validation failure responses, and create-alignment assertions at the HTTP boundary.
doneWhen: PATCH /api/issues/{issueId} is exposed and returns HTTP 200 with the unchanged IssueSummaryDto transport contract.
Update validation rejects empty payloads, blank or out-of-range titles, over-limit description, over-limit label lists, blank label items, and overlapping label operations before downstream calls.
At least one edge-case test proves description='' is accepted as an effective update and at least one edge-case test proves overlap rejection happens before service delegation.

info: IssuesController PATCH method logs issueId plus titlePresent, descriptionProvided, addLabelCount, and removeLabelCount before delegation and logs issueId after mapping the response.
warn: None beyond the existing GlobalExceptionHandler validation warning path.

slices.title: Update Service Contract
slices.goal: Add the provider-agnostic update orchestration contract and service delegation as an isolated small slice.
implementationTasks: Add IssueSummary updateIssue(UpdateIssueInput input) to IssuesPort.
Add IssuesService.updateIssue(UpdateIssueInput input) with structured start/completion logging and direct delegation to issuesPort.updateIssue(input).
Keep the update flow single-call only; no existence prefetch, no read-after-write, and no AsyncComposer use.
Extend IssuesServiceTest with update delegation coverage and adjust IssuesApiProperties construction to pass the new nested validation configuration.
doneWhen: IssuesService exposes updateIssue(UpdateIssueInput) and delegates directly to the new port method.
No orchestration read-before-write composition is introduced for the update path.
At least one service test proves the port result is returned unchanged and one test proves integration exceptions propagate unchanged.

info: IssuesService.updateIssue logs operation start with issueId plus effective field counts and logs completion with issueId.

Implement GitLabIssuesAdapter.updateIssue(UpdateIssueInput input) using PUT to the project-scoped issue URI, resource label update issue, and the existing GitLabIssueResponse -> IssueSummary mapping.

error: Reuse existing adapter transport-failure error logging via mapTransportFailure for the update issue resource.
